channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Abandoned untaxed and no parking permit, 13th May
item.description:
White Tipper truck on Mauritius Road SE10. Think abandoned as has a flat tyre. Rubbish/waste (not sure if safe) in the back (not secured or covered) blowing round. Duration 2 months.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mauritius Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bike on Cowper Street, 13th May
item.description:
Abandoned bike that hasn’t been touched in a while opposite 28 Cowper Street

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32-37, Cowper Street, Finsbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 199 (I think but could be 201), 13th May
item.description:
Mattress
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Willesden Lane, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Faded double yellow lines on the corner of Glendale Mews with Westgate Road, 13th May
item.description:
Double yellow lines faded such that they are ignored.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glendale Mews, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light not working, 13th May
item.description:
Street light not working in a very dark, bushy area with poor pavement likely to cause a fall or be dangerous
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Appledore Crescent, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street lights not on full power/dim, 13th May
item.description:
Lighting on Palace View between the junction with Murray Av and Rafford Way toward the Rafford Way end, not working properly. 3 of them are very dim, as if on reduced power, and with the overhanging large trees, the area is very dark at night.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Palace View, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 13th May
item.description:
2 double mattresses dumped on busy pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bramhope Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There is indeed a dead fox on the side of the road nearest the Coldharbour Leisure Centre., 13th May
item.description:
I think the summary probably says it all. It’s been there about three days,
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 620, Sidcup Road, Coldharbour, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Dead animal in the carriageway or footway

item.title: Dumped waste, 13th May
item.description:
Residents dumping waste outside their property

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kennington Park Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Non statutory use of drop kerb access way outside 124 Hampton road ig1 1pr, 13th May
item.description:
Non statutory use of driveway for over a long period of time Decommission dropped kerb as it’s causing problems
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bulk waste on pavement, 13th May
item.description:
Bulk waste on pavement not in bin area where there is a sign that says no rubbish dumping

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): McCoid Way, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dirty used nappies, 13th May
item.description:
Used nappies on grass verge Don't just collect and dispose Make enquiries to the resident who is leaving the mess
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carlton Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street lamp not working, 13th May
item.description:
Street lamp not working outside 83 Charlton Road. This is third time I’ve had to report the same street lamp in last six months.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Booking bike lanes, 13th May
item.description:
The bike lands were booked constantly by the garage at 86 Druid Street. Most every time riding the bike through, sometime even booking whole long bike lane from near Tanner stree to Arnold Estate. This has been happening since the garage 86 Druid Street open oits business as a delivery company. Lately, the lorry from other company even bloke both way, as a cyclist, I was force to go out the bike lane to ride on the main road facing the cars coming to me. Please tackle this long time going disgraceful and selfish issue , thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tanner Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Remove, 13th May
item.description:
Remove, blocking the entry

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victoria Park Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping - wardrobe, 13th May
item.description:
Yet another fly tipping incident here

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Reighton Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins being left on pavement every week, can’t pass with wheelchairs/buggies, 13th May
item.description:
Bins always keep overflowing into pavement, isn’t accessible for wheelchair users or those with buggies, they have space to keep bins off the pavements. This has been going on for over a year

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lordship Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: An abandoned vehicle has been in the parking lot, 13th May
item.description:
A Silver Vauxhall Astra vehicle has been abandoned in the corner of the parking lot in our community for several weeks now and has not move at all.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Aspen Green, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lights being blocked by trees along the whole of Park Hill, need cutting back severely. Not safe for women to walk down at night by themselves as practically no light, 13th May
item.description:
The whole of Park Hill at night is unsafe due to lack of light due to street lamps being blocked by trees. I’ve attached a picture but would be worth someone coming out to inspect at night around 9pm to see how dark and unsafe it is for anyone to walk, especially female members of the neighbourhood.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park Hill, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree fallen, 13th May
item.description:
Tree fallen in the middle of the road opposite peak hill, causing an obstruction, also caused a fence to break
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kirkdale, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
